sources.title: OCR A Level Further Mathematics A H245 specification

statements.statementText: This A level qualification builds on the skills, knowledge and understanding set out in the whole GCSE subject content for mathematics for first teaching from 2015 and in the GCE A Level subject content for mathematics for first teaching from 2017.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r0-b1-l0,p9-r0-b1-l1,p9-r0-b1-l2,p9-r0-b1-l3,p9-r0-b1-l4, 2b

statements.statementText: In general, the content of A Level Further Mathematics ‘Stage 1’ only assumes knowledge from A Level Mathematics ‘Stage 1’.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r0-b3-l0,p9-r0-b3-l1,p9-r0-b3-l2, 2b

statements.statementText: Occasionally knowledge and skills from the content of A Level Mathematics ‘Stage 2’ which are not in A Level Further Mathematics ‘Stage 1’ are assumed; this is indicated in the relevant content statements.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r0-b3-l2,p9-r0-b3-l3,p9-r0-b3-l4,p9-r0-b3-l5,p9-r0-b3-l6, 2b

statements.statementText: The content is separated into five areas: Pure Core, Statistics, Mechanics, Discrete Mathematics and Additional Pure Mathematics.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r0-b4-l0,p9-r0-b4-l1,p9-r0-b4-l2, 2b

statements.statementText: All learners must study the Pure Core and at least two of the optional areas.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r0-b4-l2,p9-r0-b4-l3, 2b

statements.statementText: Centres are free to teach the content in the order most appropriate to their learners’ needs.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r0-b4-l4,p9-r0-b4-l5, 2b

statements.statementText: Sections 4, 5, 6, 7 and 8 cover the pure core, statistics, mechanics, discrete mathematics and additional pure mathematics content of A Level Further Mathematics.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r4-b0-l0,p9-r4-b0-l1,p9-r4-b0-l2,p9-r4-b0-l3, 2b

statements.statementText: In our mathematics specifications (H230 and H240) we have used the numbering 1, 2 and 3 to cover the pure mathematics, statistics and mechanics sections in order to facilitate the co-teaching of both qualifications.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r1-b0-l0,p9-r1-b0-l1,p9-r1-b0-l2,p9-r1-b0-l3,p9-r4-b0-l3, 2b

statements.statementText: The italic text in the content statements provides examples and further detail of the requirements of this specification.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r3-b0-l0,p9-r3-b0-l1,p9-r3-b0-l2, 2b

statements.statementText: All exemplars contained in the specification under the heading “e.g.” are for illustration only and do not constitute an exhaustive list.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r3-b0-l2,p9-r3-b0-l3,p9-r3-b0-l4,p9-r3-b0-l5, 2b

statements.statementText: All of this content is assumed, but will only be explicitly assessed where it appears in this specification.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r0-b1-l5,p9-r0-b1-l6,p9-r0-b1-l7, 2b

statements.statementText: The heading “i.e.” is used to denote a complete list.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r3-b0-l5,p9-r3-b0-l6, 2b

statements.statementText: For the avoidance of doubt an italic statement in square brackets indicates content which will not be tested.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r3-b0-l6,p9-r3-b0-l7,p9-r3-b0-l8, 2b

statements.statementText: The expectation is that some assessment items will require learners to use two or more content statements without further guidance.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r3-b1-l0,p9-r3-b1-l1,p9-r3-b1-l2, 2b

statements.statementText: Learners are expected to have explored the connections between their optional areas and the Pure Core.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r3-b1-l2,p9-r3-b1-l3,p9-r3-b1-l4, 2b

statements.statementText: Learners may be required to demonstrate their understanding of the Pure Core content, and/or the content of A Level Mathematics (H240), within the optional papers, but that content will not be explicitly assessed.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r3-b1-l4,p9-r3-b1-l5,p9-r3-b1-l6,p9-r3-b1-l7,p9-r3-b1-l8, 2b

statements.statementText: Learners are expected to be able to use their knowledge to reason mathematically and solve problems both within mathematics and in context.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r3-b2-l0,p9-r3-b2-l1,p9-r3-b2-l2, 2b

statements.statementText: Content that is covered by any statement may be required in problem solving, modelling and reasoning tasks even if that is not explicitly stated in the statement.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r3-b2-l3,p9-r3-b2-l4,p9-r3-b2-l5,p9-r3-b2-l6, 2b

statements.statementText: Problem solving, proof and mathematical modelling will be assessed in further mathematics in the context of the wider knowledge which students taking A Level further mathematics will have studied.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r3-b3-l0,p9-r3-b3-l1,p9-r3-b3-l2,p9-r3-b3-l3, 2b

statements.statementText: In Pure Core (section 2c) learners will extend and deepen their knowledge of proof, algebra, functions, calculus, vectors and differential equations studied in A Level Mathematics.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r5-b0-l0,p9-r5-b0-l1,p9-r5-b0-l2,p9-r5-b0-l3, 2b

statements.statementText: They will also broaden their knowledge into other areas of pure mathematics that underpin the further study of mathematics and other numerate subjects with complex numbers, matrices, polar coordinates and hyperbolic functions.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r5-b0-l3,p9-r5-b0-l4,p9-r5-b0-l5,p9-r5-b0-l6,p9-r5-b0-l7, 2b

statements.statementText: This is a linear qualification.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r0-b2-l0, 2b

statements.statementText: The content is arranged by topic area and exemplifies the level of demand across two stages.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r0-b2-l0,p9-r0-b2-l1,p9-r0-b2-l2, 2b

statements.statementText: The content for each area is shown in sections 2c-2g in two columns, demonstrating the progression across each topic.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r0-b2-l2,p9-r0-b2-l3,p9-r0-b2-l4, 2b

statements.statementText: When this course is being co-taught with AS Level Further Mathematics A (H235) the ‘Stage 1’ column indicates the common content between the two specifications and the ‘Stage 2’ column indicates content which is particular to this specification.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r0-b2-l4,p9-r0-b2-l5,p9-r0-b2-l6,p9-r0-b2-l7,p9-r0-b2-l8,p9-r0-b2-l9, 2b

statements.statementText: Statements have a unique reference code.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r0-b2-l10,p9-r0-b2-l9, 2b

statements.statementText: For ease of comparison, planning and co-teaching the ‘Stage 1’ content statements in this specification have reference codes corresponding to the same statements in OCR’s AS Level in Further Mathematics A (H235).
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r0-b2-l10,p9-r0-b2-l11,p9-r0-b2-l12,p9-r0-b2-l13,p9-r0-b2-l14, 2b

statements.statementText: The content in these statements is identical, but the exemplification may differ as appropriate to the qualification.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 9, line IDs p9-r0-b2-l14,p9-r0-b2-l15,p9-r0-b2-l16, 2b

statements.statementText: In Statistics (section 2d) learners will explore the theory which underlies the statistics content in A Level Mathematics, as well as extending their tool box of statistical concepts and techniques.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r1-b0-l0,p10-r1-b0-l1,p10-r1-b0-l2,p10-r1-b0-l3, 2d-intro

statements.statementText: This area covers probability involving combinatorics, probability distributions for discrete and continuous random variables, hypothesis tests and confidence intervals for a population mean, χ-squared tests, non-parametric tests, correlation and regression.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r1-b0-l3,p10-r1-b0-l4,p10-r1-b0-l5,p10-r1-b0-l6,p10-r1-b0-l7,p10-r1-b0-l8, 2d-list

statements.statementText: In Mechanics (section 2e) learners extend their knowledge of particles, kinematics and forces from A Level Mathematics, using their extended pure mathematical knowledge to explore more complex physical systems.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r1-b1-l0,p10-r1-b1-l1,p10-r1-b1-l2,p10-r1-b1-l3,p10-r1-b1-l4, 2e-intro

statements.statementText: The area covers dimensional analysis, work, energy, power, impulse, momentum, centres of mass, circular motion and variable force.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r1-b1-l4,p10-r1-b1-l5,p10-r1-b1-l6, 2e-list

statements.statementText: Discrete Mathematics (section 2f) is the part of mathematics dedicated to the study of discrete objects.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r1-b2-l0,p10-r1-b2-l1,p10-r1-b2-l2, 2f-def

statements.statementText: Areas studied include counting, graphs and networks, algorithms, critical path analysis, linear programming, and game theory.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r1-b2-l5,p10-r1-b2-l6,p10-r1-b2-l7,p10-r1-b2-l8, 2f-list

statements.statementText: Learners will study both pure mathematical structures and techniques and their application to solving real-world problems of existence, construction, enumeration and optimisation.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r1-b2-l2,p10-r1-b2-l3,p10-r1-b2-l4,p10-r1-b2-l5, 2f-study

statements.statementText: In Additional Pure Mathematics (section 2g) learners will broaden and deepen their knowledge of pure mathematics, studying both discrete and continuous topics which form the foundation of undergraduate study in mathematics and mathematical disciplines.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r1-b3-l0,p10-r1-b3-l1,p10-r1-b3-l2,p10-r1-b3-l3,p10-r1-b3-l4, 2g-intro

statements.statementText: This area covers recurrence relations, number theory, group theory, the vector product, surfaces and partial differentiation.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r1-b3-l5,p10-r1-b3-l6,p10-r1-b3-l7, 2g-list

statements.statementText: Suggested applications of technology to teaching and learning may be found in the introduction to each content area.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r2-b1-l0,p10-r2-b1-l1,p10-r2-b1-l2, p10-r2-b1-l0

statements.statementText: Learners are permitted to use a scientific or graphical calculator for all papers.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r2-b2-l1,p10-r2-b2-l2, p10-r2-b2-l1

statements.statementText: Calculators are subject to the rules in the document Instructions for Conducting Examinations, published annually by JCQ (www.jcq.org.uk).
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r2-b2-l2,p10-r2-b2-l3,p10-r2-b2-l4,p10-r2-b2-l5, p10-r2-b2-l2

statements.statementText: It is expected that calculators available in the assessment will include the following features: • An iterative function such as an ANS key.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r2-b3-l0,p10-r2-b3-l1,p10-r2-b3-l2,p10-r2-b3-l3, p10-r2-b3-l0-1

statements.statementText: It is expected that calculators available in the assessment can perform calculations, including inversion, with matrices up to at least order 3 × 3.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r2-b3-l0,p10-r2-b3-l1,p10-r2-b3-l4,p10-r2-b3-l5,p10-r2-b3-l6,p10-r2-b3-l7, p10-r2-b3-l0-2

statements.statementText: It is expected that calculators available in the assessment will include the following features: • The ability to compute summary statistics and access probabilities from the binomial and normal distributions.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r2-b3-l0,p10-r2-b3-l1,p10-r2-b3-l10,p10-r2-b3-l11,p10-r2-b3-l8,p10-r2-b3-l9, p10-r2-b3-l0-3

statements.statementText: Allowable calculators can be used for any function they can perform.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r2-b4-l0,p10-r2-b4-l1, p10-r2-b4-l0

statements.statementText: 1. Candidates are advised to write down explicitly any expressions, including integrals, that they use the calculator to evaluate.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r2-b5-l0,p10-r3-b0-l0,p10-r3-b0-l1,p10-r3-b0-l2, p10-r2-b5-l0

statements.statementText: 2. Candidates are advised to write down the values of any parameters and variables that they input into the calculator. Candidates are not expected to write down data transferred from question paper to calculator.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r4-b0-l0,p10-r5-b0-l0,p10-r5-b0-l1,p10-r5-b0-l2,p10-r5-b0-l3,p10-r5-b0-l4, p10-r4-b0-l0

statements.statementText: 3. Correct mathematical notation (rather than “calculator notation”) should be used; incorrect notation may result in loss of marks.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r6-b0-l0,p10-r7-b0-l0,p10-r7-b0-l1,p10-r7-b0-l2, p10-r6-b0-l0

statements.statementText: Learners will be given a Formulae Booklet in each assessment, which includes the formulae given for OCR’s A Level in Mathematics A. See section 5e for the content of this booklet.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r8-b0-l1,p10-r8-b0-l2,p10-r8-b0-l3,p10-r8-b0-l4, p10-r8-b0-l1

statements.statementText: It is assumed that learners will have access to appropriate technology when studying this course such as mathematical and statistical graphing tools and spreadsheets.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r1-b4-l1,p10-r1-b4-l2,p10-r1-b4-l3,p10-r1-b4-l4, tech-access

statements.statementText: The primary use of technology at this level is to offload computation and visualisation, to enable learners to investigate and generalise from patterns.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r1-b4-l10,p10-r1-b4-l7,p10-r1-b4-l8,p10-r1-b4-l9, tech-purpose

statements.statementText: Learners are not expected to be familiar with any particular software, but they are expected to be able to use their calculator for any function it can perform, when appropriate.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r1-b4-l10,p10-r1-b4-l11,p10-r2-b0-l0,p10-r2-b0-l1,p10-r2-b0-l2, tech-unfamiliar

statements.statementText: When embedded in the mathematics classroom, the use of technology can facilitate the visualisation of certain concepts and deepen learners’ overall understanding.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r1-b4-l4,p10-r1-b4-l5,p10-r1-b4-l6,p10-r1-b4-l7, tech-visual

statements.statementText: Determine This command word indicates that justification should be given for any results found, including working where appropriate.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 11, line IDs p11-r11-b1-l11,p11-r11-b1-l12,p11-r11-b1-l13,p11-r11-b1-l14, command-word-determine

statements.statementText: Find, Solve, Calculate These command words indicate, while working may be necessary to answer the question, no justification is required. A solution could be obtained from the efficient use of a calculator, either graphically or using a numerical method.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 11, line IDs p11-r11-b3-l0,p11-r11-b3-l1,p11-r11-b3-l2,p11-r11-b3-l3,p11-r11-b3-l4,p11-r11-b3-l5, command-word-find-solve-calc

statements.statementText: Give, State, Write down These command words indicate that neither working nor justification is required.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 11, line IDs p11-r11-b4-l0,p11-r11-b4-l1,p11-r11-b4-l2, command-word-give-state-write

statements.statementText: Prove Learners are given a statement and must provide a formal mathematical argument which demonstrates its validity. A formal proof requires a high level of mathematical detail, with candidates clearly defining variables, correct algebraic manipulation and a concise conclusion.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 11, line IDs p11-r11-b0-l0,p11-r11-b0-l1,p11-r11-b0-l2,p11-r11-b0-l3,p11-r11-b0-l4,p11-r11-b0-l5,p11-r11-b0-l6, command-word-prove

statements.statementText: Show that Learners are given a result and have to show that it is true. Because they are given the result, the explanation has to be sufficiently detailed to cover every step of their working.
examplesText: e.g. Show that the curve y = x ln x has a stationary point (1/e, −1/e).
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 11, line IDs p11-r11-b1-l0,p11-r11-b1-l1,p11-r11-b1-l10,p11-r11-b1-l2,p11-r11-b1-l3,p11-r11-b1-l4,p11-r11-b1-l5,p11-r11-b1-l6,p11-r11-b1-l7,p11-r11-b1-l8,p11-r11-b1-l9, command-word-show-that

statements.statementText: Verify A clear substitution of the given value to justify the statement is required.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 11, line IDs p11-r11-b2-l0,p11-r11-b2-l1,p11-r11-b2-l2, command-word-verify

statements.statementText: Exact An exact answer is one where numbers are not given in rounded form.
examplesText: Find the exact solution of ln x = 2. The correct answer is e², not 7.389 056.
Find the exact solution of 3x = 2. The correct answer is x = 2/3 or x = 0.6̇, not x = 0.67 or similar.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 11, line IDs p11-r10-b2-l0,p11-r10-b2-l1,p11-r10-b2-l2,p11-r12-b0-l3,p11-r12-b0-l4,p11-r12-b0-l5,p11-r12-b0-l6,p11-r12-b0-l7,p11-r12-b0-l8, exact-def

statements.statementText: The answer will often contain an irrational number such as √3, e or π and these numbers should be given in that form when an exact answer is required.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 11, line IDs p11-r10-b2-l2,p11-r10-b2-l3,p11-r10-b2-l4,p11-r10-b2-l5, exact-irrational

statements.statementText: The use of the word ‘exact’ also tells learners that rigorous (exact) working is expected in the answer to the question.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 11, line IDs p11-r12-b0-l0,p11-r12-b0-l1,p11-r12-b0-l2, exact-working

statements.statementText: Pre-release There is no pre-released large data set for this qualification.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 11, line IDs p11-r0-b0-l0,p11-r0-b0-l1,p11-r0-b0-l2, prerelease

statements.statementText: In general, learners should show sufficient detail of their working to indicate that a correct method is being used.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 11, line IDs p11-r10-b1-l0,p11-r10-b1-l1,p11-r10-b1-l2, showing-working

statements.statementText: Simplifying expressions It is expected that learners will simplify algebraic and numerical expressions when giving their final answers, even if the examination question does not explicitly ask them to do so.
examplesText: 80 × (√3/2) should be written as 40√3.
½(1 + 2x)⁻¹ᐟ² × 2 should be written as either (1 + 2x)⁻¹ᐟ² or 1/√(1 + 2x).
ln 2 + ln 3 − ln 1 should be written as ln 6.
The equation of a straight line should be given in the form y = mx + c or ax + by = c unless otherwise stated.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 11, line IDs p11-r0-b1-l0,p11-r0-b1-l1,p11-r0-b1-l2,p11-r0-b1-l3,p11-r0-b1-l4,p11-r0-b2-l0,p11-r0-b3-l0,p11-r1-b0-l0,p11-r1-b1-l0,p11-r1-b2-l0,p11-r2-b0-l0,p11-r2-b0-l1,p11-r2-b0-l2,p11-r3-b0-l0,p11-r3-b1-l0,p11-r4-b0-l0,p11-r4-b1-l0,p11-r5-b0-l0,p11-r5-b0-l1,p11-r5-b0-l2,p11-r6-b0-l0,p11-r7-b0-l0,p11-r8-b0-l0,p11-r9-b0-l0,p11-r9-b0-l1,p11-r9-b0-l2, simplifying-expressions

statements.statementText: In this question you must show detailed reasoning. When a question includes this instruction learners must give a solution which leads to a conclusion showing a detailed and complete analytical method. Their solution should contain sufficient detail to allow the line of their argument to be followed. This is not a restriction on a learner’s use of a calculator when tackling the question, e.g. for checking an answer or evaluating a function at a given point, but it is a restriction on what will be accepted as evidence of a complete method.
notesText: The displayed examples illustrate the minimum analytical steps required; equivalent exact forms and different intermediate steps are acceptable.
examplesText: Express −4 + 2i in modulus–argument form: √20(cos 2.68 + i sin 2.68), showing the modulus and argument calculations.
Evaluate ∫₄⁵ 1/√(x² − 16) dx and show the limiting substitution needed to obtain ln 2.
Solve 2x³ − 11x² + 22x − 15 = 0, showing a real root or factor before factorising the cubic.

statements.statementText: Hence When a question uses the word ‘hence’, it is an indication that the next step should be based on what has gone before. The intention is that learners should start from the indicated statement.
examplesText: e.g. You are given that f ( x ) = 2 x 3 - x 2 - 7 x + 6 . Show that ^ x - 1 h is a factor of f ( x ) . Hence find the three factors of f ( x ) .
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 12, line IDs p12-r7-b1-l0,p12-r7-b1-l1,p12-r7-b1-l2,p12-r7-b1-l3,p12-r7-b1-l4,p12-r7-b1-l5,p12-r7-b1-l6,p12-r7-b1-l7,p12-r7-b2-l0, H245-command-words

statements.statementText: Hence or otherwise This is used when there are multiple ways of answering a given question. Learners starting from the indicated statement may well gain some information about the solution from doing so, and may already be some way towards the answer. The command phrase is used to direct learners towards using a particular piece of information to start from or to a particular method. It also indicates to learners that valid alternate methods exist which will be given full credit, but that they may be more time-consuming or complex.
examplesText: e.g. Show that ( cos x + sin x ) 2 = 1 + sin 2 x for all x. Hence, or otherwise, find the derivative of ^ cos x + sin x h 2 .
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 12, line IDs p12-r8-b0-l0,p12-r8-b0-l1,p12-r8-b0-l10,p12-r8-b0-l11,p12-r8-b0-l12,p12-r8-b0-l13,p12-r8-b0-l14,p12-r8-b0-l2,p12-r8-b0-l3,p12-r8-b0-l4,p12-r8-b0-l5,p12-r8-b0-l6,p12-r8-b0-l7,p12-r8-b0-l8,p12-r8-b0-l9, H245-command-words

statements.statementText: Be able to find and use matrices to represent linear transformations in 2-D.
notesText: Includes:
• reflection in either coordinate axis and in the lines y = ± x
• rotation about the origin (defined by the angle of rotation i, where the direction of positive rotation is taken to be anticlockwise)
• enlargement centre the origin (defined by the the scale factor)
• stretch parallel to either coordinate axis (defined by the invariant axis and scale factor)
• shear parallel to either coordinate axis (defined by the invariant axis and the image of a transformed point).
Includes the terms “object” and “image”.

statements.statementText: c) Understand the meaning of the terms “walk”, “trail”, “path”, “cycle” and “route”.
notesText: A “walk” is a set of arcs where the end vertex of one is the start vertex of the next. A “trail” is a walk in which no arcs are repeated.
